weather_line: Clearing skies over Diagon Alley · uneasy in the banking district

stories.headline: Christine Gives Her Answer: The Goblin Election Will Never Be the Same, and Neither Will Ragnok's Filing System
body: At nine forty-seven on Wednesday morning, Christine submitted her character reference for Chief Account-Keeper Ragnok to the Gringotts Election Board. It was one page. It was signed. It contained seven sentences that sources describe as 'accurate, measured, and somehow devastating to both candidates simultaneously.' The Board read it in silence. A quill dropped. Nobody picked it up.

The reference neither endorsed Ragnok nor dismissed him. It described, with the quiet precision of someone who has audited a soul without a spreadsheet, exactly what kind of goblin he is: thorough, proud, occasionally impossible, and — in the final sentence, which the Board has since requested clarification on — 'the only Account-Keeper in this institution whose word I would stake a vault on.' Legal is still arguing about whether that is an endorsement. It is not, technically, an endorsement. It is something considerably harder to argue with.

Griphook the Younger, upon receiving his copy of the reference, requested a forty-eight-hour recess. The Board granted thirty minutes. Ragnok has not spoken publicly, but witnesses report that he straightened his collar, turned away from the corridor window, and said nothing for quite some time — which, for Ragnok, is the closest thing to moved that anyone has documented in writing. The election proceeds to final arguments Thursday. Christine has already returned to her actual work, which was waiting, respectfully, in a tidy pile.

stories.headline: Election Board Convenes Thursday; Security Upgraded After Last Year's Incident with the Enchanted Abacus
body: The Gringotts Election Board has confirmed that Thursday's final arguments will be held in Chamber Three, the largest available room after Chamber One was quietly retired following a structural disagreement with an enchanted abacus in last year's budget review. Seating is limited. A waiting list exists. The waiting list also has a waiting list.

In a related development, Griphook the Younger's campaign office has filed a formal objection to the reference document's use of the word 'vault' in a metaphorical context, arguing that the word 'vault' is a Gringotts technical term and its metaphorical deployment constitutes an unauthorized audit. The Board has set aside an additional fifteen minutes to discuss this. Observers estimate it will take four.

stories.headline: Steve Completes the Thing He Started; House Confirms It Is 'Better Than Before, Somehow'
body: Steve finished the project on Wednesday afternoon. The exact nature of the project has been described by three separate household sources as 'the thing with the bracket,' 'the situation in the back,' and 'you'll see.' The result is, by all accounts, solid, level, and finished in a manner that suggests the house has been waiting for it since roughly 2019 and simply hadn't said anything.
The enchanted Jeep, parked nearby throughout, is reported to have approved. It has not required any attention whatsoever this week, which Steve acknowledges may be its way of showing respect.

stories.headline: Steve's Map Has Acquired a Second Bookmark; the Jeep Has Noticed
body: Sources close to the household confirm that the Appalachian trail map currently occupying the corner of Steve's workbench now features two bookmarks: one in the established Jellico corridor and a second, newer one, pressed into a fold approximately forty miles east and described only as 'worth a look.' The Jeep's tyre pressure, last checked Tuesday, remains optimal. This is considered significant.

Christine has not yet been formally briefed. Steve has rehearsed the opening of this briefing twice, which is once more than usual and suggests the destination is either very good or involves a water crossing. Possibly both.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was observed sitting approximately eighteen inches from the biscuit tin for the duration of the morning in what she has described, under gentle questioning, as 'a coincidence.' No biscuits were unaccounted for. Lucy would like this noted.

Ellie: Spent a productive Wednesday making friends with the new bracket Steve installed, sniffing it three times, and arriving at the conclusion that it has always been there. She seems pleased with this discovery and has moved on.

Jersey: Having identified Christine's chair as the warmest available surface during her brief mid-morning break, Jersey executed a textbook lap occupation in under four seconds and has submitted a formal request that the break be extended indefinitely.
founding_editor_note: One page, seven sentences, and the whole room went quiet. That's good writing. I built the presses for exactly that kind of story. — D.

in_brief.sport: Wednesday Quidditch League standings delayed after the Scoring Charm confused a total of six goals with six galleons and briefly enriched the Keeper.
in_brief.forecast: Clearing by midmorning, with a strong likelihood of something significant on Thursday; bring an umbrella if you are a goblin, and confidence if you are not.

storyline_updates.title: The Goblin Election
storyline_updates.summary: Christine delivered her reference Wednesday morning — one page, seven sentences, conditional in form and decisive in effect. The Election Board convenes Thursday for final arguments. Ragnok straightened his collar. Griphook the Younger requested a recess. The reference described Ragnok with precision that made it impossible to argue with and impossible to call a simple endorsement. Arc closed: Christine handled it exactly as Christine handles everything — on her own terms, before anyone had finished asking the question.

storyline_updates.title: The Second Bookmark
storyline_updates.summary: A new arc opens quietly: Steve's trail map has acquired a second bookmark, forty miles east of Jellico, in a fold he hasn't named aloud yet. The Jeep's tyre pressure is optimal. Christine hasn't been briefed. Day 2 should cover the briefing — and her reaction to whatever Steve has found worth bookmarking twice.
